creator: carlson39@llnl.gov
text:
I just installed pam_abl from both ports/security/pam_able and by package (pkg_add -r pam_able) on three FreeBSD systems (two are running 7.2, the other is running 8.0-BETA2), and I am have this same problem as previously described. I used the same settings that Alex Keda had posted on Oct, 4,2007.

First, /var/db/pam_abl/users.db and /var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db was not created:
> ls -la /var/db/pam_abl/
total 4
drwxr-xr-x   2 root  wheel  512 Aug 27 14:20 .
drwxr-xr-x  11 root  wheel  512 Aug 27 14:20 ..

here is the output from debug.log:
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_db=/var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_purge=2d
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_rule=*:10/1h,30/1d
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_db=/var/db/pam_abl/users.db
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_purge=2d
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_rule=!root:10/1h,30/1d
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: Checking host 192.168.1.100
Aug 28 09:37:11 fone pam_abl[84093]: Checking user mcarlson

Output of pam_abl:
> pam_abl
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_db=/var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_purge=2d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_rule=*:10/1h,30/1d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_db=/var/db/pam_abl/users.db
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_purge=2d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_rule=!root:10/1h,30/1d
Failed users:
Failed hosts:

Now, after I 'touch /var/db/pam_abl/users.db ; touch /var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db':
> touch /var/db/pam_abl/users.db ; touch /var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
> ls -la /var/db/pam_abl/
total 4
drwxr-xr-x   2 root  wheel  512 Aug 28 09:40 .
drwxr-xr-x  11 root  wheel  512 Aug 27 14:20 ..
-rw-r--r--   1 root  wheel    0 Aug 28 09:40 hosts.db
-rw-r--r--   1 root  wheel    0 Aug 28 09:40 users.db

Now, I try logging in again:
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_db=/var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_purge=2d
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_rule=*:10/1h,30/1d
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_db=/var/db/pam_abl/users.db
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_purge=2d
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_rule=!root:10/1h,30/1d
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: Checking host 192.168.1.100
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db opened
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: Checking user mcarlson
Aug 28 09:40:59 fone pam_abl[84112]: /var/db/pam_abl/users.db opened

But the user and host is never added to either .db file:
> pam_abl
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_db=/var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_purge=2d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: host_rule=*:10/1h,30/1d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_db=/var/db/pam_abl/users.db
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_purge=2d
DEBUG: /usr/local/etc/pam_abl.conf: user_rule=!root:10/1h,30/1d
Failed users:
   <none>
Failed hosts:
   <none>

The db files appear to be empty:
> db41_dump /var/db/pam_abl/users.db
VERSION=3
format=bytevalue
type=btree
db_pagesize=4096
HEADER=END
DATA=END
> db41_dump /var/db/pam_abl/hosts.db
VERSION=3
format=bytevalue
type=btree
db_pagesize=4096
HEADER=END
DATA=END

Can this ticket be re-opened and re-investigated?

Thanks,
Mike Carlson
carlson39@llnl.gov
